payload.friendly_name: Unshim Wave 2 — registered-shim removals + charter deprecation-cycle closure
payload.purpose_context: Second slice of the tidy-first unshim cluster (epic #1797) per the HiC current-cycle ruling (registering shims at a version target recreates the rescinded deferral — hence full delete). Pre-planning 3-lens squad (debbie/priti/alphonso, 2026-07-03, main@47fed302d): next-shim has 3 src callers (one is a monkeypatch seam) + 77 test files/~480 refs (issue said 49 — drifted); glossary is a zero-src-caller husk; #2290 is ~4x its issue framing (19 test files + the test_charter_runtime_shim_paths.py lock-gate must retire + canonical-imports-legacy defect at charter_runtime/preflight/runner.py:36 + 4th unmarked shim charter_activate.py); runtime.next.* is ALREADY the sanctioned surface (no prerequisite work); WS1 layer rule proven NOT a blocker (no import edge) but folds in as a probe/bind WP; category_6 frozen re-exports NOT in scope (falsified claim). Risk class: live-caller re-point THEN delete; the 80 patch-string targets are the silent-no-op class Wave 1 proved real.
payload.purpose_tldr: Execute #2291 (delete the specify_cli.next + specify_cli.glossary registered shims after re-pointing all live callers to runtime.next/glossary canonical surfaces) and #2290 as FULL charter-shim deletion (re-point + delete charter_lint/freshness/preflight + adjudicate charter_activate), with fold-ins: #2326 dead-wrapper prune and the WS1 mission_runtime layer-rule bind.
